name: Ourense and Ribeiro Wine Route
description: Cycle the difficult 51.6-mile Ourense - Miño Return route, gaining 3420 feet with scenic river views and historical sights.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Barbadás traverse the green, hilly landscapes of Ourense province in Galicia, Spain. The region is characterized by its undulating terrain, river valleys, and agricultural areas, including the renowned Ribeiro wine region. Cyclists can expect a mix of ascents and descents, offering varied challenges across its network of roads. Best road cycling routes around Barbadás The most popular road cycling route is Ourense Velodrome Laps, a 16.6 miles (26.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 7 minutes to complete. This route offers an accessible option for an easy ride. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ourense and Ribeiro Wine Route, a difficult 51.6 miles (83.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the scenic wine-producing region. Local road cyclists also love the Ourense, Ribadavia, and Celanova Loop, a 80.3 miles (129.2 km) trail leading through multiple towns and varied terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 39 minutes. Road cycling around Barbadás is defined by its rolling hills, river valleys, and vineyard landscapes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to challenging long-distance rides. The routes in Barbadás are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Barbadás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Barbadás?
A: Road cycling routes around Barbadás traverse the green, hilly landscapes of Ourense province in Galicia, Spain. You can expect undulating terrain, river valleys, and agricultural areas, including the renowned Ribeiro wine region, offering a mix of ascents and descents.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Barbadás area?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Barbadás, catering to various skill levels. This includes 1 easy route, 5 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes for more experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Barbadás offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927901382/ourense-and-ribeiro-wine-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ourense and Ribeiro Wine Route&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 51.6-mile (83.0 km) path with significant elevation gain through the scenic wine-producing region. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e928015348/ourense-ribadavia-and-celanova-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ourense, Ribadavia, and Celanova Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an 80.3-mile (129.2 km) trail with varied terrain.

Q: What attractions can I see along the road cycling routes in Barbadás?
A: Many routes pass by significant landmarks. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Vella (Roman Bridge of Ourense)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of San Martín de Ourense&lt;/a&gt;. Natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Burgas Roman Baths&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, consider routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantiño Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3829291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Boutureira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling loops suitable for a day trip?
A: Yes, for a moderate day trip, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936632127/ourense-and-a-peroxa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ourense and A Peroxa Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 43.3 miles (69.6 km) with significant elevation. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936617845/ourense-and-toen-cycling-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ourense and Toén Cycling Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 32.0-mile (51.5 km) route.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Barbadás?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from rolling hills to river valleys and vineyard landscapes, which offer options for all ability levels. The scenic beauty and well-curated routes are frequently highlighted.

Q: Can I find easy road cycling routes around Barbadás?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy road cycling route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938888168/ourense-velodrome-laps" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ourense Velodrome Laps&lt;/a&gt; is an accessible 16.6-mile (26.7 km) trail, perfect for an easy ride or for those looking for a less strenuous option.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Ribeiro wine region?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927901382/ourense-and-ribeiro-wine-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ourense and Ribeiro Wine Route&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to take you through this renowned wine-producing area, offering scenic views of vineyards and agricultural landscapes.

Q: What are some notable historical sites I can visit while cycling in the region?
A: Beyond the cycling, you can explore the rich history of Ourense. Key historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Vella (Roman Bridge of Ourense)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of San Martín de Ourense&lt;/a&gt;, and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Burgas Roman Baths&lt;/a&gt;, all of which are easily accessible from various routes.

Q: Are there any thermal baths or hot springs accessible near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its thermal waters. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5017252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz and Burga de Canedo Thermal Baths&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular spots to relax after a ride. Some cycling routes pass close to these natural monuments.

Q: How popular is road cycling in Barbadás according to komoot users?
A: Road cycling around Barbadás is quite popular within the komoot community. Over 1,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, and the routes collectively hold an impressive average rating of 5.0 stars from more than 10 reviews.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Ourense - Miño Return route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a "difficult" road ride, requiring a very good level of fitness. It involves significant elevation gains, with over 1,000 meters (3,400 feet) of climbing, making it suitable for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: The Ourense - Miño Return tour typically takes around 3 hours and 52 minutes to complete for a racebike, covering a distance of approximately 83 kilometers (51.5 miles).

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ourense - Miño Return route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily composed of well-paved surfaces, ideal for racebiking. However, be prepared for some unpaved and potentially difficult segments where you might need to push your bike. The region features rugged terrain with rivers, mountains, and valleys.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers picturesque landscapes along the Miño River. You'll pass by the Beautiful Valley, the historic Monastery of San Clodio, the scenic Puente Esperela, and the Castrelo de Miño Nautical Park. The area is also known for its thermal waters and Roman-origin bridges.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Ourense - Miño Return?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is milder and more predictable. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures and more rain, making some sections less enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to access the Ourense - Miño Return route?
faqs.answer: While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, Ourense city offers various parking options. It's advisable to look for public parking facilities within Ourense, especially near the Miño River, which would provide convenient access to the start of the route.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Caminho da Geira e dos Arrieiros, Camiño Xabrés (Oeste), and the Paseo Fluvial do Miño, among others. These intersections are generally short segments.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Ourense - Miño Return route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route, dogs are generally permitted, especially if they are well-behaved and kept on a leash or in a bike trailer. However, always be mindful of local regulations and other cyclists.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Ourense - Miño Return route. It utilizes public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through charming villages, specific cafes or restaurants directly on the trail are not detailed. It's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, and plan stops in larger towns or Ourense city for meals and refreshments.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, essential gear includes a well-maintained racebike, helmet, appropriate cycling attire, sufficient water and nutrition, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tools), and navigation tools. Due to potential unpaved sections, consider tires with good puncture resistance.
